title: podman-systemd [Container] Network= does not support referencing templated containers
body:
### Issue Description

[Manual](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#network) says

> If the name ends with .container, the container will reuse the network stack of another container created by $name.container. The generated systemd service contains a dependency on $name.service. Note: the corresponding .container file must exist.

However, if your [Container] Network= references a templated container (i.e. container@instance.container), the service fails to generate at systemctl daemon-reload.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a templated container definition (e.g. container@.container).
2. Symlink an instance of this container (e.g. ln -s container@.container container@instance.container).
3. Reference it in another container (e.g. anothercontainer.container [Container] Network=container@.container or Network=container@instance.container).
4. systemctl daemon-reload


### Describe the results you received

anothercontainer.service fails to generate

### Describe the results you expected

anothercontainer.service should reference --network container:[name of container@instance.container]
